name: Pic du Midi
summary: A spectacular astronomy-and-Pyrenees viewpoint above the Tourmalet corridor, best treated as a dedicated non-race excursion.
whyItMatters: It connects directly to the Tourmalet setting and gives the mountain days a cultural/scientific anchor beyond roadside race viewing.
timeRequired: 3-4 hours minimum from La Mongie once cable-car timing, summit visit, and buffers are included.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Cable-car ticket required; advance booking strongly recommended.
pros: One of the strongest non-city cultural/natural experiences near the Tourmalet plan
Observatory and high-altitude panorama make it distinct from generic scenic overlooks
Good candidate for a clear-weather day before or after Stage 6
cons: Not a quick stop from Arreau once driving and cable-car logistics are included
Weather can make the experience poor or impossible
Race-day road closures around Tourmalet/La Mongie could make it impractical on July 9

logistics.opening_hours: Verify official cable-car operating calendar and weather status before committing.
logistics.distance_from_base: Roughly 1-1.5 hours driving from Arreau to La Mongie in normal conditions; verify current routing.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Near the Tourmalet corridor but not the selected col roadside point; access starts from La Mongie.
logistics.transit: Drive to La Mongie, then cable car.
logistics.parking: La Mongie parking required; race-week restrictions unknown.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Do not plan for Stage 6 race day unless official road closure research explicitly supports it.

timing.best_visit_window: Clear-weather non-race day in the Pyrenees.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with La Mongie or Bagneres-de-Bigorre food rather than relying on a tight summit schedule.

sources.title: Pic du Midi official site
sources.note: Official visitor, ticket, weather, and cable-car planning source.
